frame_analyses.description: The trainees are preparing to load the needle onto the instrument.
Needle is being inserted through the drain at the first mark.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Slight deviation observed from the mark.

frame_analyses.description: Beginning the first throw of the knot using the right hand.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Throw appears secure with a double wrap.

frame_analyses.description: Switching hand to perform the second throw.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Switching hand correctly for the second throw.

frame_analyses.description: Performing the third throw, switching hand again.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hand switching executed correctly; knot appears stable.

frame_analyses.description: Cutting the suture ends inside the trainer.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Cut is clean and within the required area.

frame_analyses.description: Both ends of the suture have been cut, completing the task.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Ends have been cut properly inside the trainer.

knot_assessments.notes: Double wrap visible, knot appears secure.
Hand switching was correct, knot remains tight.
Final throw completed with proper hand switch.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: The slit is completely closed, no gaps visible.

technique_summary: The task was completed successfully with minor deviations during suture placement and proper hand switching observed throughout the knot tying process.
improvement_suggestions: Focus on more precise needle entry at the marked points.
Maintain fluid hand-switching to improve efficiency.
strengths: Good execution of the surgeon's knot.
Stable and secure knot tying technique observed.
